---
title: "04 — Model Comparison, Tuning & Final Evaluation"
author: "PSTAT 134 — AI Writing Detector"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    embed-resources: true
    theme: simplex
    code-fold: show
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import sys
from pathlib import Path

PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "notebooks" else Path.cwd()
sys.path.append(str(PROJECT_ROOT))

import joblib
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import seaborn as sns
from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.model_selection import GridSearchCV
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.svm import LinearSVC

from src.features import build_tfidf_vectorizer
from src.evaluation import cv_summary, stratified_cv, test_set_report

sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
```

## Load processed train/test splits

```{python}
train_df = pd.read_csv(PROJECT_ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "train.csv")
test_df = pd.read_csv(PROJECT_ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "test.csv")
X_train, y_train = train_df["text"], train_df["generated"]
X_test, y_test = test_df["text"], test_df["generated"]
```

## Compare candidate models with shared CV folds

Every model uses the **same** stratified folds so the comparison is fair.

```{python}
candidates = {
    "logreg": Pipeline([
        ("tfidf", build_tfidf_vectorizer()),
        ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, n_jobs=-1)),
    ]),
    "naive_bayes": Pipeline([
        ("tfidf", build_tfidf_vectorizer()),
        ("clf", MultinomialNB()),
    ]),
    "linear_svm": Pipeline([
        ("tfidf", build_tfidf_vectorizer()),
        ("clf", LinearSVC()),
    ]),
    "gboost": Pipeline([
        ("tfidf", build_tfidf_vectorizer(max_features=5000)),
        ("clf", GradientBoostingClassifier(random_state=42)),
    ]),
}

cv = stratified_cv()
results = {name: cv_summary(model, X_train, y_train, cv=cv) for name, model in candidates.items()}
comparison = pd.concat(results, axis=1)
comparison
```

## Pick the best model and tune it

Edit `BEST_NAME` once you know which candidate to take forward.

```{python}
BEST_NAME = "logreg"
best_pipeline = candidates[BEST_NAME]

param_grid = {
    "tfidf__ngram_range": [(1, 1), (1, 2)],
    "tfidf__min_df": [2, 5],
    "clf__C": [0.5, 1.0, 2.0],
}

search = GridSearchCV(
    best_pipeline,
    param_grid=param_grid,
    scoring="f1_macro",
    cv=stratified_cv(),
    n_jobs=-1,
    refit=True,
)
search.fit(X_train, y_train)
search.best_params_, search.best_score_
```

## Final evaluation on the held-out test set

This is the **only** time we touch the test set.

```{python}
final_model = search.best_estimator_
report = test_set_report(final_model, X_test, y_test)

{k: v for k, v in report.items() if k not in {"confusion_matrix", "classification_report"}}
```

```{python}
print(report["classification_report"])
```

```{python}
#| label: confusion-matrix
cm = report["confusion_matrix"]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 3.5))
sns.heatmap(
    cm,
    annot=True,
    fmt="d",
    cmap="Blues",
    xticklabels=["human", "ai"],
    yticklabels=["human", "ai"],
    cbar=False,
    ax=ax,
)
ax.set_xlabel("Predicted")
ax.set_ylabel("Actual")
ax.set_title("Confusion matrix — held-out test set")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig(PROJECT_ROOT / "reports" / "figures" / "confusion_matrix.png", dpi=150)
```

## Inspect the most informative features

```{python}
#| label: top-features
vectorizer = final_model.named_steps["tfidf"]
clf = final_model.named_steps["clf"]

if hasattr(clf, "coef_"):
    feature_names = np.array(vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
    coefs = clf.coef_.ravel()
    top_ai = pd.Series(coefs, index=feature_names).nlargest(20)
    top_human = pd.Series(coefs, index=feature_names).nsmallest(20)
    pd.concat({"top AI-leaning": top_ai, "top human-leaning": top_human}, axis=1)
```

## Persist the final model

```{python}
models_dir = PROJECT_ROOT / "models"
models_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
joblib.dump(final_model, models_dir / f"{BEST_NAME}_final.joblib")
```

## Notes for the report

- Compare the CV ranking to the test-set ranking — large gaps suggest
  overfitting to the CV folds.
- Use the top features and confusion matrix to argue *why* the model is making
  the predictions it makes.
- Discuss how well any of this would generalize to AI text from a different
  generator (e.g., a newer model than the one in the dataset).
